bright_lines: Never modify anything under `skills/**` — the superlooper engine (and any other installed skill) lives there. The engine is supervised-only and is NEVER loop-built. Declare `touches: dashboard` and stay inside `dashboard/**`; any diff that reaches the `engine` area is flagged at the gate as an out-of-lane wander. The engine's real backstop is publishing: a merged engine change is inert until someone republishes through the gated `bin/install.sh`, which shows the diff and requires an explicit OK.

Never modify anything under `.superlooper/**` — it is the loop's executable config (the referee's own rulebook: gates, required checks, notify commands). A PR that edits it reprograms the loop; such changes come only through a supervised session, never a loop worker.

Never modify anything under `.github/workflows/**` — CI is the mechanical gate that judges your PR, and a worker must not edit its own referee. Workflow changes come only through a supervised session.
No AI or LLM call anywhere inside the dashboard or its server: every button is an existing mechanical verb (label change, comment, issue create). No model calls, no standing seat.
The server binds 127.0.0.1 only — it can write GitHub labels (William's word), so it must never be reachable off the machine. Never bind 0.0.0.0 or a public interface.
